1976 Italian crime comedy film

***Hit Squad*** ([Italian](/wiki/Italian_language "Italian language"): ***Squadra antifurto***) is a 1976 Italian "[poliziottesco](/wiki/Poliziotteschi "Poliziotteschi")"-[comedy film](/wiki/Comedy_film "Comedy film") directed by [Bruno Corbucci](/wiki/Bruno_Corbucci "Bruno Corbucci"). It is the second chapter in the Nico Giraldi film series starred by [Tomas Milian](/wiki/Tomas_Milian "Tomas Milian").[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-ccc-4)

## Plot

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hit_Squad_(film)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Plot")]

Marshal Nico Giraldi, a member of the anti-theft team, deals with criminals specializing in apartment and car thefts. Among these criminals are Blinds, a small-time thief prone to confessing, Zagaja, a stuttering thief, and Sicilian brothers Rosario and Salvatore Trapani. During a theft at an American villa (owned by a former CIA agent), the thieves acquire documents revealing a bribery system involving significant figures in the United States, known as Deal Zebra Point.

After contacting US emissary Ralf Douglas to demand money for the return of the documents, Blinds is killed. The murder of Zagaja alarms Marshal Giraldi, who suspects that Douglas had been robbed of something crucial. To uncover the truth, Giraldi tracks the Trapani brothers, who have been in contact with Douglas's emissaries. The attempt fails, and the brothers are killed. However, an emissary of Douglas leaves a clue that leads Giraldi to New York. There, he tracks down Douglas and has him arrested.

## Cast

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hit_Squad_(film)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Cast")]

* [Tomas Milian](/wiki/Tomas_Milian "Tomas Milian"): Nico Giraldi
* [Robert Webber](/wiki/Robert_Webber "Robert Webber"): Mr. Douglas
* [Lilli Carati](/wiki/Lilli_Carati "Lilli Carati"): Vanessa
* [Giuseppe Pambieri](/wiki/Giuseppe_Pambieri "Giuseppe Pambieri"): Tapparella
* [Giuliana Calandra](/wiki/Giuliana_Calandra "Giuliana Calandra"): Lt. Ciampini
* [Toni Ucci](/wiki/Toni_Ucci "Toni Ucci"): Filotto
* [Olimpia Di Nardo](/w/index.php?title=Olimpia_Di_Nardo&action=edit&redlink=1 "Olimpia Di Nardo (page does not exist)"): Olimpia Trippetta
* [Massimo Vanni](/wiki/Massimo_Vanni "Massimo Vanni"): Gargiulo
* [John P. Dulaney](/w/index.php?title=John_P._Dulaney&action=edit&redlink=1 "John P. Dulaney (page does not exist)"): Ballarin
* [Vittorio Stagni](/wiki/Vittorio_Stagni "Vittorio Stagni"): Er Zagaja
* [Enzo Pulcrano](/wiki/Enzo_Pulcrano "Enzo Pulcrano"): Salvatore Trapanese
* [Benito Stefanelli](/wiki/Benito_Stefanelli "Benito Stefanelli"): Avv. Gorniani
* [Bombolo](/wiki/Bombolo "Bombolo"): Er Trippa
* [Mimmo Poli](/wiki/Mimmo_Poli "Mimmo Poli"): Vittorio Raganelli (Il Musulmano)
* [Giancarlo Badessi](/wiki/Giancarlo_Badessi "Giancarlo Badessi"): Maniac
* [Anna Bonaiuto](/wiki/Anna_Bonaiuto "Anna Bonaiuto"): Cameriera
* Tony Morgan: Gnappetta
* [Nello Pazzafini](/wiki/Nello_Pazzafini "Nello Pazzafini"): Capitano della nave

## Release

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hit_Squad_(film)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Release")]

*Hit Squad* was released on October 29, 1976 in Italy where it was distributed by [Titanus](/wiki/Titanus "Titanus").[[2]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti2013177-2) The film grossed a total of 1,825,316,810 [Italian lire](/wiki/Italian_lira "Italian lira") on its theatrical release.[[2]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti2013177-2)
